top_signals.title: Baltic Dry Index Up for 4th Day
top_signals.summary: The Baltic Exchange’s dry bulk freight index, which monitors rates for ships carrying dry bulk commodities, advanced for the fourth session on Friday, rising about 2.5% to its highest since June 19 at 2,717 points, mainly supported by the larger vessel segment. The capesize index, which typically transports 150,000-ton cargoes including iron ore and coal, ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Easyjet board reaches agreement over £5.2bn Castlelake takeover
top_signals.summary: The board of Easyjet is poised to accept a £5bn takeover bid by private equity firm Castlelake, in a move that could add the airline to a growing list of blue-chip companies that have unveiled plans to quit the London Stock Exchange since the start of the year. The offer of £6.90 per share, the [...]
